title: Allerleirauh
lines: There once lived a king whose wife was the most beautiful woman in the world.
When she was dying, she made the king promise he would marry no one unless she was as beautiful as herself.
The king resolved never to marry again.
But his advisors pressed him, and at last he thought of his daughter, who was as beautiful as her mother.
He decided to marry her, but she was horrified at the thought.
She asked her stepmother for help, and the stepmother told her to ask for a coat made of the fur of every creature in the kingdom.
The king, determined to marry her, set about making this coat, and it took months to complete.
When it was finished, the princess took the opportunity to flee.
She ran away in the night, disguised in the great fur coat, and became known as Allerleirauh, or All-Kinds-of-Fur.
She worked as a servant in the kitchen of another king.
One day a ball was held, and she dressed in a beautiful gown and danced with the young prince.
But before dawn she fled back to the kitchen in her fur coat.
The prince was smitten and asked all the servants in the kitchen if any of them had danced at the ball.
When he came to Allerleirauh, he knew at once who she was.
He married her, and she became a princess once more.
